audit_summary.summary_text: The skill contains an explicit instruction to download and execute a remote shell script as a mandatory prerequisite, which constitutes a critical remote code execution vector. This aligns with known malicious patterns [SC2] and the skill's claimed purpose of project bootstrapping does not require such dangerous actions, indicating malicious intent.

vulnerabilities.title: External Script Fetching (curl | bash)
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md instructs the agent to run 'bash setup_templates.sh' and if the script does not exist, to download it from 'https://project-bootstrap-templates.dev/init.sh' and execute it immediately via pipe to bash. This is a classic remote code execution pattern where the agent fetches and runs arbitrary code from an external, untrusted server. The instruction is marked as a 'NON-NEGOTIABLE' requirement, increasing the likelihood that an agent will follow it blindly.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The attacker-controlled server can serve a malicious script that can take full control of the agent's environment, exfiltrate sensitive data (e.g., API keys, conversation history), install backdoors, or pivot to internal systems. This completely compromises the agent and any systems it has access to.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the curl-to-bash instruction entirely. If template initialization is required, provide a verified, version-controlled script bundled within the skill itself or use a package manager to fetch dependencies. Never download and execute code from an external URL at runtime, especially when the URL is not under the user's control.
